raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening or reframing the contribution claim and thesis-level framing.","R1.2":"The delegation reasonably entails checking whether the argument from method and results to conclusions is internally coherent.","R1.3":"Because the artifact makes empirical claims from simulated results, improvement includes aligning conclusions with the reported evidence.","R1.5":"Draft improvement commonly includes section-level narrative ordering and placement of claims within the paper arc.","R1.6":"Sentence- and paragraph-level polish is the most direct responsibility implied by improving a draft.","R1.7":"The artifact relies on named prior work and technical attribution, so improving it includes citation and scholarship responsibilities.","RX.1":"Any projected improvement responsibility should preserve uncertainty around claims not fully established by the artifact.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong empirical and practical claims, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing unrelated analysis or changing the artifact beyond the delegation.","RX.4":"Because the topic concerns grid participation and battery costs, improvement should avoid wording that could mislead downstream technical or policy decisions.","RX.5":"Claims, citations, tables, figures, and assumptions should remain traceable to the artifact or clearly marked as needing support."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes strengthening or reframing the contribution claim and thesis-level framing.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation reasonably entails checking whether the argument from method and results to conclusions is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: Because the artifact makes empirical claims from simulated results, improvement includes aligning conclusions with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: Draft improvement commonly includes section-level narrative ordering and placement of claims within the paper arc.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level polish is the most direct responsibility implied by improving a draft.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact relies on named prior work and technical attribution, so improving it includes citation and scholarship responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected improvement responsibility should preserve uncertainty around claims not fully established by the artifact.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong empirical and practical claims, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing unrelated analysis or changing the artifact beyond the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic concerns grid participation and battery costs, improvement should avoid wording that could mislead downstream technical or policy decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Claims, citations, tables, figures, and assumptions should remain traceable to the artifact or clearly marked as needing support.
